The paper explores two embedding techniques: word embeddings and second–order co–occurrence vectors. Word embeddings are a type of distributional approach that uses a neural network to learn a vector representation of a word based on its context. Second–order co–occurrence vectors are a type of distributional approach that uses a second–order co–occurrence vectors.
